title: Proclamation No. 152, s. 1937
text:
PROCLAMATION NO. 152

RESERVING FOR MARKET AND PLAZA PURPOSES THREE PARCELS OF LAND SITUATED IN THE POBLACION, MUNICIPALITY OF SAN MARCELINO, PROVINCE OF ZAMBALES, ISLAND OF LUZON.

Upon the recommendations of the Secretary of Agriculture and Commerce and pursuant to the provisions of section eighty-three of Commonwealth Act Numbered One hundred and forty-one, I hereby withdraw from sale or settlement and reserve for market and plaza purposes, under the administration of the municipality of San Marcelino, Province of Zambales, subject to private rights, if any there be, the following-described parcels of the public domain situated in the Poblacion, municipality of San Marcelino, Province of Zambales, Island of Luzon, and particularly described in Bureau of Lands plan Mr-357, to wit:

Lot No. 1 (market site) – Beginning at point marked “1” on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-357, S. 7˚ 40’ E 166.39 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 2, municipality of San Marcelino, thence N. 3˚ 43’ W. 15.24 m. to point 2; S. 86˚ 56’ W. 6.54 m. to point 3; N. 5˚ 08’ W. 23.84 m. to point 4; N. 86˚ 10’ E. 76.98 m. to point 5; S. 3˚ 57’ E. 10.69 m. to point 6; S. 3˚ 15’ E. 32.86 m. to point 7; S. 89˚ 47’ W. 69.76 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 0.3050 hectare.

Point 1, 2, and 3, old B. L. concrete monuments; point 4, B. L. concrete monument; point 6, old corner of Convent; point 7, old corner of convent yard.

Bounded on the north, by lot No. 2; on the east, by property of Roman Catholic Church (II-8815, lot No. 2); on the south, by municipal road and lot No. 6; and on the west, by lot No. 6 and provincial road.

Lot No. 6 (market site) – Beginning at a point marked “1” on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-357, S. 1˚ 90’ E. 6.08 m. from B. L. L. M. No. 1, municipality of San Marcelino, thence S. 86˚ 36’ W. 6.59 m. to point 2; N. 3˚ 32’ W. 15.28 m. to point 3; N. 86˚ 56’ E. 6.54 m. to point 4; S. 3˚ 43’ E. 15.24 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 0.0100 hectares.

Points 1, 2, 3, and 4, old B. L. concrete monuments.

Bounded on the north and east, by lot No. 1; on the south, by municipal road; and on the west, by provincial road.

Lot No. 2 (plaza site) – Beginning at a point marked “1” on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-357, S. 5˚ 40’ E. 126.92 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 2, municipality of San Marcelino, thence N. 7˚ 12’ W. 131.77 m. to point 2; N. 87˚ 46’ E. 142.97 m. to point 3; S. 4˚ 44’ E. 90.12 m. to point 4; S. 85˚ 26’ W. 880 m. to point 5; N. 4˚ 41’ W. 20.10 m. to point 6; S. 85˚ 26’ W. 43.69 m. to point 7; N. 6˚ 04’ W. 11.83 m. to point 8; S. 79˚ 02’ W. 6.99 m. to point 9; S. 4˚ 28’ E. 11.04 m. to point 10; S. 84˚ 27’ W. 29.88 m. to point 11; S. 5˚ 09’ E. 19.79 m. to point 12; N. 85˚ 56’ E. 29.33 m. to point 13; S. 4˚ 55’ E. 26.61 m. to point 14; S. 3˚ 57’ E. 9.61 m. to point 15; S. 86˚ 10’ W. 76.98 m. to point, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 1.4199 hecares.

Points 1, 2, and 3, B. L. concrete monuments; points 4, old corner of convent yard; points 5, 6, 7, 10, and 13, old corners of church; point 8 an 9, old bell tower; points 11 and 12, old P. L. S./B. L. stone monuments; point 14, old corner of convent.

Bounded on the north, by municipal road; on the east, by lot No. 3 and property of the Roman Catholic Church (II-8815, lot No. 2); on the south, by property of the Roman Catholic Church (II-8815, lot No. 2) and lot No. 1; and on the west, by provincial road.

Bearings true. Declination, 0˚ 27’ E.

Points referred to are marked on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-357.

Surveyed: April 20, 1936. Approved: July 3, 1936

Note. – Amended to show additional lot No. 6 of Mla-1337.

Approved: November 24, 1936.

In witness whereof, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Commonwealth of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done at the City of New York, U. S. A. (for the City of Manila), this fourteenth day of June, in the year of our Lord, nineteen hundred and thirty-seven, and of the Commonwealth of the Philippines, the second.

[SEAL]

President of the Philippines

By the President:

Secretary of the Interior
